file.content:
FROM mcr.microsoft.com/devcontainers/base:jammy

USER vscode

# install: claude code
RUN curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash

USER root

# install: 
#   yq
RUN wget https://github.com/mikefarah/yq/releases/download/v4.45.1/yq_linux_amd64 -O /usr/local/bin/yq &&\
    chmod +x /usr/local/bin/yq

COPY rootfs/ /
RUN chown -R vscode:vscode /home/vscode
RUN find /usr/local/bin -type f -exec chmod +x {} \;

USER vscode

CMD ["sleep", "infinity"]

content.text:
Super straightforward, Sean. Two options:

### Option 1: One line in the Dockerfile (simplest)

The base image is `mcr.microsoft.com/devcontainers/base:jammy` (Ubuntu 22.04), so it's just an `apt-get`:

```dockerfile
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y sqlite3 && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
```

Drop that in with the other `USER root` installs. Done.

### Option 2: Dev container feature

There's no official devcontainer feature for sqlite3 in the `ghcr.io/devcontainers/features/` collection. There are some community ones floating around (`ghcr.io/devcontainers-extra/features/sqlite3` etc.) but given you already have a comment about a broken `devcontainers-extra` feature in your config, I'd avoid that path.

**Recommendation:** Just add the `apt-get` line to the Dockerfile. It's a ~1MB package with zero configuration — a feature would be overkill. Want me to add it?

input.new_string:
# install:
#   sqlite3, yq
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y sqlite3 && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
RUN wget https://github.com/mikefarah/yq/releases/download/v4.45.1/yq_linux_amd64 -O /usr/local/bin/yq &&\
    chmod +x /usr/local/bin/yq

# install:
#   sqlite3, yq
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y sqlite3 && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
RUN wget https://github.com/mikefarah/yq/releases/download/v4.45.1/yq_linux_amd64 -O /usr/local/bin/yq &&\
    chmod +x /usr/local/bin/yq
toolUseResult.originalFile:
FROM mcr.microsoft.com/devcontainers/base:jammy

USER vscode

# install: claude code
RUN curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash

USER root

# install: 
#   yq
RUN wget https://github.com/mikefarah/yq/releases/download/v4.45.1/yq_linux_amd64 -O /usr/local/bin/yq &&\
    chmod +x /usr/local/bin/yq

COPY rootfs/ /
RUN chown -R vscode:vscode /home/vscode
RUN find /usr/local/bin -type f -exec chmod +x {} \;

USER vscode

CMD ["sleep", "infinity"]

1	# Coco Log — Standing Research Instrument
2	#
3	# One entry per unit of work handled directly by Coco (the interactive
4	# collaborator) rather than dispatched as an autonomous commission.
5	# Parallel to the commission log — together they capture the full
6	# picture of how work is routed in the system.
7	#
8	# An entry is created for each commit (or tight cluster of commits
9	# toward a single goal) that Coco produces during a session.
10	#
11	# Fields:
12	#   session        — Coco's Claude session ID (join key to archived transcripts)
13	#   date           — date the work was done (YYYY-MM-DD)
14	#   item           — short description of the work (one line)
15	#   commissionable — whether this work could have been an autonomous commission
16	#   justification  — why it can't be commissioned (required when commissionable is false)
17	#
18	# Justifications (required when commissionable: false):
19	#   interactive     — required real-time iteration with Sean
20	#   sanctum         — sanctum/experiment/meta work outside the commission boundary
21	#   decisional      — output was a decision, not an artifact

file.content:
# Coco Log — Standing Research Instrument
#
# One entry per unit of work handled directly by Coco (the interactive
# collaborator) rather than dispatched as an autonomous commission.
# Parallel to the commission log — together they capture the full
# picture of how work is routed in the system.
#
# An entry is created for each commit (or tight cluster of commits
# toward a single goal) that Coco produces during a session.
#
# Fields:
#   session        — Coco's Claude session ID (join key to archived transcripts)
#   date           — date the work was done (YYYY-MM-DD)
#   item           — short description of the work (one line)
#   commissionable — whether this work could have been an autonomous commission
#   justification  — why it can't be commissioned (required when commissionable is false)
#
# Justifications (required when commissionable: false):
#   interactive     — required real-time iteration with Sean
#   sanctum         — sanctum/experiment/meta work outside the commission boundary
#   decisional      — output was a decision, not an artifact

entries:
  - session: 8d1d17b8-8db3-4a6a-8382-4df6c73b594e
    date: 2026-04-04
    item: "Delete legacy quality-review scripts, update all references to instrument-review.sh"
    commissionable: false
    justification: sanctum

  - session: 8d1d17b8-8db3-4a6a-8382-4df6c73b594e
    date: 2026-04-04
    item: "Create coco-log standing research instrument, add git identity and commit process to Coco agent instructions"
    commissionable: false

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Coco Log — Standing Research Instrument
#
# One entry per unit of work handled directly by Coco (the interactive
# collaborator) rather than dispatched as an autonomous commission.
# Parallel to the commission log — together they capture the full
# picture of how work is routed in the system.
#
# An entry is created for each commit (or tight cluster of commits
# toward a single goal) that Coco produces during a session.
#
# Fields:
#   session        — Coco's Claude session ID (join key to archived transcripts)
#   date           — date the work was done (YYYY-MM-DD)
#   item           — short description of the work (one line)
#   commissionable — whether this work could have been an autonomous commission
#   justification  — why it can't be commissioned (required when commissionable is false)
#
# Justifications (required when commissionable: false):
#   interactive     — required real-time iteration with Sean
#   sanctum         — sanctum/experiment/meta work outside the commission boundary
#   decisional      — output was a decision, not an artifact
